title: Aux-Eye Perception Observation
description: Single source of truth for a structured aux-eye observation. An aux-eye observation is produced by: (1) camera_capture.py writes a frame JPEG and its sha256; (2) a multimodal agent reads that frame and fills the content fields; (3) the observation is validated against this schema and cross-checked by tools/aux-eye/verify_aux_eye.py. FIELD RELIABILITY (see .omo/evidence/vision-probe.md): the always-producible status/handoff fields (source_frame_sha256, visible, failure_reason) are REQUIRED because they can be produced regardless of whether the execution session has real image-reading capability. The vision-dependent CONTENT fields (scene, objects, confidence) are OPTIONAL: in a non-vision session they may be absent or empty; in a vision-capable session they should be populated. This keeps one contract valid under both kinds of session and never forces the vision step to fabricate content. This schema is intentionally morphology-agnostic: it describes generic scene perception and contains no robot-specific fields.

source_frame_sha256.description: Cryptographic proof that the agent read exactly the frame the capture tool wrote: the SHA-256 hex digest (64 hex chars) of the source frame file. Computed by the calling agent/harness (or camera_capture.py), NOT by the vision model. verify_aux_eye.py re-computes sha256(frame) and asserts it equals this value (frame-identity gate). Independent of vision capability, therefore always required.

visible.description: Whether a usable image was actually perceived. true = the frame was read and its content is available in this observation; false = no usable content could be read (dark/occluded/empty/no-vision-capability). This is a fail-safe status signal: when in doubt the producer must emit false rather than claim true. Always producible, therefore required.

failure_reason.description: Why the frame was not usable, or 'none' when it was. Invariant (also enforced by if/then/else below and by verify_aux_eye.py): visible=false pairs with failure_reason != 'none'; visible=true pairs with failure_reason == 'none'. 'dark' = too dark / underexposed; 'occluded' = lens blocked or content hidden; 'empty' = no discernible content / no vision capability; 'none' = frame usable, no failure. Always in-enum and producible, therefore required.

scene.description: OPTIONAL (vision-dependent). High-level description of the scene. May be absent or have an empty subject in a non-vision session.

subject.description: Short natural-language description of the primary subject / gist of the frame (e.g. 'a white mug on a wooden desk'). Empty string is allowed when nothing could be read.

objects.description: OPTIONAL (vision-dependent). Distinct objects perceived in the frame. May be an empty array when nothing could be read.

name.description: Short label for the object (e.g. 'mug', 'qr code', 'cable').

confidence.description: Per-object self-assessed confidence in [0,1]. NOT calibrated and NOT comparable across models — a self-report, not a probability.

OPTIONAL (vision-dependent). Top-level self-assessed overall confidence in this observation, in [0,1]. IMPORTANT: this is self-assessed, NOT calibrated, and NOT comparable across models or runs. Treat it as a coarse signal only, never as a probability.
